patch.value: Recovery bundle mines flag names from usage-error prose: scope errors like '--rebuild and --clear apply only to graph index' yield missing:--clear + suggested_retry appending the other invalid flag

Observed with pm graph analyze --rebuild (2026-07-20 manual E2E): the usage error '--rebuild and --clear apply only to graph index.' produces recovery.missing: --clear and suggested_retry: 'pm graph analyze --rebuild --clear' — the exact opposite of the fix. The recovery generator extracts flag tokens from arbitrary error MESSAGE prose and treats them as missing required flags. Sibling of the arity lineage (pm-6y58/pm-9qcr fixed arity, not provenance): flags named in a scope/mutual-exclusion explanation are not missing inputs. Fix direction: only derive missing-flag suggestions from structured metadata (contract-declared required flags), never from message text; scope/exclusivity usage errors should suggest removing the offending flag or switching subcommand.

Scope/mutual-exclusion usage errors produce no missing-flag rows and no suggested_retry that adds another invalid flag; missing-flag derivation consults only structured contract metadata; regression covers the graph --rebuild/--clear and --save-baseline scope errors

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Duplicate check 2026-07-20: searched 'recovery bundle suggested_retry misleading flag' across all statuses — pm-6y58 (GH-500, built-in flag arity) and pm-9qcr (GH-507, extension contract arity) both FIXED and cover arity only; no existing item covers missing-flag PROVENANCE (mining flag tokens from error prose). Filed from the pm-ju83/6x7o/7ob5 graph-platform lane manual E2E.

author: maintainer-agent
message: Graph hygiene: attach discovered recovery UX defect to canonical CLI capability and scripting-contract lineage; remains open and unclaimed outside this PR.
